user_message: Summarize what happened with Adatum Corporation and give me the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-06 09:04 EST
Subject: forecast prep - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with forecast prep. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
Date: 2024-02-18 09:42 EST
Subject: URGENT: forecast prep Issue at Adatum Corporation

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about forecast prep. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-01 16:53 EST
Subject: Re: forecast prep Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with forecast prep. What are the next steps from your side?

Meeting: forecast prep Discussion - Adatum Corporation
Date: 2024-02-17
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), David (Adatum Corporation)

[00:02:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on forecast prep from our last session.

[00:07:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:10:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:15:00] David (Adatum Corporation): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:19:00] David (Adatum Corporation): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:21:00] David (Adatum Corporation): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
